untranslatable_reason: This signal describes a portfolio-level capital allocation rule based on market capitalization weighting (fourth root transformation) and liquidity constraints. It is not a traditional price-action or technical indicator-based entry/exit signal. The rule requires: (1) external market cap data not in the allowed indicators list, (2) cross-asset allocation logic (comparing positions across multiple stocks), and (3) position-sizing calculations rather than entry/exit triggers. The allowed indicators are single-asset, price/volume-based only and cannot express multi-asset allocation mathematics or market cap transformations.
translation_notes: Signal is fundamentally a portfolio construction rule, not a tradeable entry/exit condition. It specifies: allocate weight ∝ fourth_root(market_cap), enforce max 10:1 weight ratio, verify liquidity. These operations require external data (market cap), cross-sectional comparisons, and mathematical transformations outside the scope of single-asset technical indicator rules. Recommend implementing this as a separate position-sizing module in the backtest engine rather than as a signal rule.
